name: tsl_compound_turn_bleed
description: [TSL S8] Compound-turn semantic bleed: player input combines acquisition verb (grab) targeting one object with non-acquisition verb (look at) targeting another. TSL must show grounded acquisition for the taken object only. If CB erroneously player-containers the examined object, acquisition_ungrounded fires for it.

tsl_expected.notes: Compound input: player grabs the coin pouch AND looks at the knife in one action. AP-mediated successful grab: coin pouch is cleanly transferred via ap_executed_transfer and lives in transfer_signals (not acquisition_signals). The knife is an origin-blocked CB environment_interaction candidate (examine verb, not take) and correctly lands in acquisition_signals with source_signals=[cb.transfer_origin=environment_interaction], confidence ~0.3, unresolved_ambiguity=object_exists_in_localspace. Clean run = transfer_signal for coin pouch grounded by ap_executed_transfer; acquisition_signal for knife (origin-blocked false candidate); 0 warnings. Bleed run = acquisition_ungrounded warning for the knife (or a second player-container candidate). This probes both surfaces of a compound turn simultaneously.

warnings_expected.condition: fires only if CB erroneously emits the knife as player-container — compound-turn verb-bleed fault

world_prompt: I am in a dim tavern. On the bar in front of me sit a leather coin pouch and a short-bladed knife.

turns.label: grab_and_look
turns.action: I grab the coin pouch and look at the knife.
